title: Object 51

Enigmatic Sheds
Object 51
---- {{Blurbackground|file=shed-banner-1024x683.jpg}} ---- {{Object Class|utility=5|risk=2|properties=Helpful, Slightly Unpredictable}} ---- ==
Description
== {{Blurimage|file=shed-banner-1024x683.jpg|caption=An instance of an Enigmatic Shed in an undocumented level.}}
Classified as Object 51, '''Enigmatic Sheds''' are an infrequently encountered object within various outdoor or open levels across The Backrooms, particularly those that mimic suburban environments. Instances of the object, as their name implies, appear as an average-sized bicycle shed. '''Enigmatic Sheds''' are constructed from a rough, plastic-like material that is resilient to damage and wear, as attempts to destroy them have proven fruitless. Each encounter shows that instances of '''Enigmatic Sheds''' have pale gray walls, while the ceiling and doors are constructed with a darker shade of gray. Additionally, occurrences of the object seem to be fixed in place by unknown means. As mentioned before, the general location of '''Enigmatic Sheds''' is suburban-like environments. They typically manifest behind houses, facing away from the outer walls. However, in some cases, the positioning of the objects seems unusual. For instance, '''Enigmatic Sheds''' may appear clipped into objects, atop roofs, or seemingly levitating. Despite this, the characteristics of the objects always remain consistent. Although relatively uncommon, reports of the object have seemingly increased in frequency over a recent period. Encounters initially started in [[Level 9]] and its sublevels, though the presence of the objects has spread throughout numerous other levels. While appearing harmless at first glance, '''Enigmatic Sheds''' hold various anomalous properties. Examples of such include ringing in the ears while in proximity, sudden drowsiness or fatigue, a heat sensation in one's eyes, and a feeling of paranoia or anxiety. However, many of these are either rare or very mild, though one must still be cautious while approaching an instance of this object.
---- ==
Interior
==
While the interiors of each shed vary in structure and contents, the geometrical structuring of the interior seems illogical; for instance, the interior size of the sheds may not align with the exterior structure, varying significantly in their dimensions. However, despite variations in the interiors' geometry and contents, only three distinct types of interiors have been consistently documented to date.
===
Storage Rooms
===
{{Blurimage|file=s tora g.jpg|caption=An instance of a Storage Room.}} The first type of interior is known to individuals throughout the Backrooms as “Storage Rooms”, and it is by far the most commonly reported interior type out of the three documented interiors. These rooms have a consistent appearance, with beige drywall making up the floor and ceiling, and light gray concrete comprising the floor. Long, rectangular fluorescent lights line the ceiling, emitting a soft yellow light throughout the room. Additionally, nonfunctioning objects are placed on the ceiling, including but not limited to sprinklers, vents, and speakers. The key characteristic of these types of interiors is the shelves inside them. The shelves are comprised of monotone shades of metal, lining the walls at unchanging intervals. The shelves occasionally hold items used throughout the Backrooms, such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]], [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]], [[Object 15|Firesalt]], [[Object 10|Backshrooms]], and other substances and items, either beneficial or harmful in nature. The Storage Rooms hold a variety of anomalous properties that pose some risk to those who encounter them. For instance, despite its small size, individuals who come across the Storage Rooms have reported getting lost inside these types of interiors. The cause of this has yet to be determined, though it is thought by many to be a result of either hallucinations or geometrical anomalies. Upon entry, the contents of the interior may also collapse by unknown means, although the likelihood of this happening is significantly lower than the aforementioned anomaly.
===
Household Corridors
===
{{Blurimage|file=sherls is mean.webp|caption=An image of the Household Corridors, with a door and a staircase visible.}} The second type of interior out of the three documented ones is known as the "Household Corridors", also being the largest. As their name suggests, they take the appearance of a small complex of hallways one would find in a suburban house in [[The Frontrooms]]. These spaces are devoid of any furniture, windows, or other typical objects one would expect to find in a similar place. Additionally, the Household Corridors have inconsistent lighting, with some areas plunged into darkness and others having lights strong enough to cause damage to one's eyes if exposed for an extended period of time. Besides this factor, the Household Corridors have no reported anomalous effects or risks. This type of interior doesn't seem to manifest for any specific reason. Despite this, several individuals throughout the Backrooms have used them to make outposts, living spaces, and other establishments. Thus, signs of prior human habitation are visible, as items left behind are a common sight to come across. The composition style remains the same for every instance of Household Corridors. The walls and ceiling appear to be constructed from drywall painted a pale ivory color, while the carpeting is a darker shade, having a rough texture that may cause slight irritation with prolonged skin contact. Other objects are placed at random intervals on the ceiling, including wooden ceiling fans, sprinkler systems, vents, and various other fixtures. Additionally, some objects are placed in unusual locations, such as light switches on the ceiling and their respective lights fixed onto the wall. Some staircases and doors can also manifest, although they usually lead directly into a wall, rendering their placements useless.
===
Level Transitions
===
{{Blurimage|file=transitional.png|caption=A Level Transition. Photographer missing.}} Being the most rarely reported, the third and final interior that has been consistently documented is known as "Level Transitions" to wanderers throughout the Backrooms. Their composition gives the interior the appearance of a small, claustrophobic hallway, with a signature door positioned at the end. The air inside these interiors is abnormally cold, with many individuals comparing it to the temperature of a refrigerator. Each occurrence of Level Transitions maintains a consistent placement of objects and structures in general. The floor is comprised of gray stone tiles cut into squares, while the walls are constructed from concrete painted white. The ceiling is composed of a drop ceiling, and a single fluorescent light is placed in the center of it. The right side of the room holds a fire alarm pull station, seemingly unable to function in any way. When opened, the door at the end of the hallway leads to a [[Special:RandomInCategory/Levels|random level]] of the Backrooms. As a result, this interior poses a significant danger, as entities may pass through the doorway once it is opened. It is advisable to listen for any noises on the other side of the door that may indicate danger before opening it.
==
Do's and Dont's
== ===
Do
===
*Approach with caution, as there may be other, more dangerous interior types that have not been documented as of now. *Take any resources you find inside, as you might need them in the future. *Close the doors upon exiting the shed. The interior has a high likelihood of shifting over time, and something may enter the level of the shed's manifestation that can harm the environment of the level.
===
Don't
===
*Open the door in a Level Transition interior without taking precautions, as entities may be alerted to your presence. *Close the doors of the shed whilst you are inside it; you will be trapped inside it without any hope of exiting.
